# Overview

We need a "Session Runner" page that will look inside of the `/sessions` directory for files that end in ".log". These are generated log files from previous sessions including new-line delimited commands that were sent and received by the robot.

Example contents of a log file:

```
1766156514.928036|10.10.11.181|RX|PING 3~
1766156514.928036|10.10.11.181|TX|ACK 0 3~
1766156800.678543|10.10.11.181|TX|QUAD 11 (-0.50,0.58) (0.40,0.61) (-0.30,-0.04) (0.32,-0.02)~
1766156800.781178|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 8 11~
1766156880.310783|10.10.11.181|TX|STOP 22~
1766156887.842632|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 20 22~
1766156894.828213|10.10.11.181|TX|ZERO 25~
1766156894.921023|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 22 25~
1766156901.644975|10.10.11.181|TX|LEFT_SHOULDER 27~
1766156901.769532|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 24 27~
1766156905.513287|10.10.11.181|TX|QUAD 29 (-0.48,0.60) (0.48,0.60) (-0.28,-0.02) (0.32,-0.02)~
1766156905.596076|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 26 29~
1766156912.288533|10.10.11.181|RX|PING 28~
1766156912.304294|10.10.11.181|TX|ACK 31 28~
1766156917.936057|10.10.11.181|TX|QUAD 33 (-0.53,0.59) (0.41,0.62) (-0.27,-0.03) (0.30,-0.01)~
1766156917.988141|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 30 33~
1766156921.514272|10.10.11.181|TX|RIGHT_SHOULDER 35~
1766156921.629900|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 32 35~
1766156933.425098|10.10.11.181|TX|QUAD 39 (-0.52,0.58) (0.41,0.58) (-0.32,-0.06) (0.27,-0.06)~
1766156933.636244|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 36 39~
1766156945.316244|10.10.11.181|TX|LEFT_HIP 43~
1766156945.444871|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 40 43~
1766156958.549910|10.10.11.181|TX|OUT_OF_SESSION 46~
1766156973.030863|10.10.11.181|TX|ACK 49 46~
1766157379.687801|10.10.11.181|TX|SPIN_ME 94~
1766157379.814029|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 29 94~
1766157385.828693|10.10.11.181|TX|MOVE_UPI 96~
1766157385.935853|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 31 96~
1766157392.052802|10.10.11.181|TX|SPIN_MEC 97~
1766157392.141033|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 33 97~
1766157574.223091|10.10.11.181|TX|STOP 148~
1766157392.141033|10.10.11.181|RX|ACK 38 148~
```

There should be buttons for each file found in the `/sessions` directory that when clicked will read each item in that file and display them as buttons. So there should be buttons for each log file and then when clicked, buttons should appear for each command in that log file.

When a command button is clicked, that command should be sent to the Communication Manager via the existing WebSocket connection just like commands from the "Command Runner" page. Then those commands should be forwarded to the robot client as normal.

There should be "Start", "Stop" and "Step" buttons that allow the user to control the sending of commands from the selected log file.

There should be a "Loop" checkbox that when checked will cause the session to restart from the beginning when it reaches the end.

# Requirements

- Buttons for each log file in the `/sessions` directory.
- When a log file button is clicked, read the file and create buttons for each command in that file.
- When a command button is clicked, send that command to the Communication Manager via WebSocket.
- Ensure the UI is user-friendly and clearly indicates which log file and command is being interacted with.
- The "Start" button should begin sending commands from the selected log file at a regular interval (e.g., one command every second).
- The "Stop" button should halt the sending of commands but retain the current position in the log file.
- The "Step" button should send the next command in the log file without starting continuous sending.
- The "Loop" checkbox should enable or disable looping of the session when the end of the log file is reached.
